questions.question:
Consider the following pseudo code, where x and y are positive integers.

begin
  q := 0
  r := x
  while r >= y do
  begin
    r := r - y
    q := q + 1
  end
end

The post condition that needs to be satisfied after the program terminates is

A. {r = qy + r ∧ r < y}
B. {x = qy + r ∧ 0 <= r ∧ r < y}
C. {x = qy + r ∧ 0 < r ∧ r <= y}
D. {q + 1 < r - y / y > 0}
questions.answer: B. {x = qy + r ∧ 0 <= r ∧ r < y}
questions.to_find: The post-condition that must hold after the pseudo-code terminates

understand.plain: The pseudo-code performs integer division of x by y using repeated subtraction. It keeps subtracting y from r and counting how many times it does so in q. At the end, q holds the quotient and r holds the remainder of x divided by y. The post-condition must capture both what the variables mean and the termination condition of the loop.

keywords.explain: A logical assertion that must be true after a program or loop terminates; it describes the final state of the variables.
keywords.example: After dividing 17 by 5, post-condition: x=17, q=3, r=2, so x = 5*3 + 2 = 17.

keywords.explain: A condition that holds before and after every iteration of a loop, helping prove correctness.
keywords.example: In this loop x = q*y + r is maintained throughout every iteration.

keywords.explain: Dividing integer x by y to get quotient q and remainder r such that x = q*y + r with 0 <= r < y.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='420' height='180'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='400' height='50' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='32' class='lbl ink'>Initial: q=0, r=x (e.g. x=17, y=5)</text><text x='20' y='52' class='sub mut'>x = q*y + r is invariant throughout</text><rect x='10' y='75' width='400' height='45' rx='8' class='hi'/><text x='20' y='97' class='lbl ink'>Loop ends when r < y</text><text x='20' y='115' class='sub mut'>Post-condition: x = q*y + r  AND  0 <= r < y</text><rect x='10' y='132' width='400' height='38' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='152' class='sub ink'>Example: 17 = 3*5 + 2, q=3, r=2, 0 <= 2 < 5 ✓</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Shows the pseudo-code starts with q=0, r=x. The loop maintains x = q*y + r throughout. After termination the loop guard r >= y is false, meaning r < y, giving post-condition x = q*y + r AND 0 <= r < y.

given.aim: Find the post-condition that must be true after the repeated-subtraction loop terminates.

terms.meaning: Positive integers; x is the dividend, y is the divisor
terms.connects: The loop divides x by y using subtraction

terms.meaning: Quotient — number of times y was subtracted from r
terms.connects: q counts iterations; at end q = floor(x/y)

terms.meaning: Remainder — what is left after all complete subtractions
terms.connects: r < y at termination (loop guard failed); together x = q*y + r

terms.meaning: Loop runs as long as r is at least y
terms.example: r=7 >= y=5, so loop continues; r=2 < 5, so loop stops
terms.connects: Negation of guard (r < y) becomes part of post-condition

given.plan: The loop invariant is x = q*y + r (holds initially and after every iteration). When the loop exits, the guard r >= y is false, i.e., r < y. Since r is obtained by subtracting positive y from non-negative values, r >= 0 always. Combining: x = q*y + r AND 0 <= r < y.

steps.title: Identify the loop invariant
steps.apply: Initially q=0 and r=x, so x = 0*y + x = x. TRUE. After one iteration r becomes r-y and q becomes q+1, so q*y + r becomes (q+1)*y + (r-y) = q*y + y + r - y = q*y + r = x. Invariant preserved. So at all points, x = q*y + r.
steps.note: This is the division algorithm identity: dividend = quotient * divisor + remainder.

steps.title: Apply the negated loop guard at termination
steps.apply: The loop exits when r < y. Since r starts at x (positive) and only decreases by y each time, and y > 0, r never goes negative (the guard r >= y prevents that). So 0 <= r < y at termination.
steps.note: Combining invariant and negated guard: x = q*y + r AND 0 <= r AND r < y.

steps.title: Match with options
steps.apply: Option B states: {x = qy + r ∧ 0 <= r ∧ r < y}. This matches exactly. Option A incorrectly equates r with itself (tautology). Option C uses r <= y which is wrong (remainder must be strictly less than divisor). Option D is nonsensical.
steps.note: The post-condition encodes exactly the Euclidean division theorem.

solution.result: B. {x = qy + r ∧ 0 <= r ∧ r < y}

questions.formula_note: Euclidean division theorem: for positive integers x and y, there exist unique non-negative integers q and r such that x = q*y + r and 0 <= r < y.

questions.to_find: The loop invariant that holds before every iteration of the while loop

understand.plain: This function uses fast (binary) exponentiation to compute X^Y. It uses the ideas: if b is even, a^b = (a^2)^(b/2); if b is odd, a^b = a * a^(b-1). The variable res accumulates partial results. The loop invariant relates res, a, b to the original X and Y — it captures that the product res * a^b always equals X^Y throughout the loop.

keywords.explain: A condition that is true before the loop starts, true before each iteration, and true when the loop ends.
Computes a^b in O(log b) time by squaring: if b even, a^b=(a^2)^(b/2); if b odd, a^b=a*(a^(b-1)).
After each branch of the if-else, the invariant res*a^b must still equal X^Y.
keywords.example: Even case: res unchanged, a->a^2, b->b/2. New res*a^b = res*(a^2)^(b/2) = res*a^b = X^Y. Preserved.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='440' height='200'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='420' height='40' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='20' y='35' class='lbl ink'>Invariant: res * a^b = X^Y (always true)</text><rect x='10' y='60' width='200' height='60' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='80' class='tag ink'>Even case (b % 2 == 0)</text><text x='20' y='98' class='sub ink'>a = a*a, b = b/2</text><text x='20' y='114' class='sub mut'>res*(a^2)^(b/2) = res*a^b ✓</text><rect x='225' y='60' width='205' height='60' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='235' y='80' class='tag ink'>Odd case (b % 2 != 0)</text><text x='235' y='98' class='sub ink'>res = res*a, b = b-1</text><text x='235' y='114' class='sub mut'>(res*a)*a^(b-1) = res*a^b ✓</text><rect x='10' y='135' width='420' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='155' class='sub ink'>Loop ends when b=0. Then res*a^0 = res*1 = res = X^Y.</text><text x='20' y='175' class='sub ink'>So return res correctly returns X^Y.</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Diagram showing the loop invariant res*a^b = X^Y. In the even case, a is squared and b halved, preserving the invariant. In the odd case, res absorbs one factor of a and b decreases by 1, also preserving the invariant. When b reaches 0, res = X^Y.

given.aim: Identify the loop invariant that is true before every iteration of the while loop in the fast exponentiation function.

terms.meaning: Accumulator that collects partial results; starts at 1
terms.example: In computing 2^5: starts 1, after odd step: res=2
terms.connects: Part of invariant; when b=0, res holds the final answer X^Y

terms.meaning: Current base; starts at X and gets squared in even steps
terms.connects: a^b combined with res always equals X^Y

terms.meaning: Current exponent; starts at Y and decreases each iteration
terms.connects: Loop runs while b != 0; determines which branch to take

terms.meaning: The target value being computed, never changes
terms.connects: The invariant asserts res*a^b always equals this constant

given.plan: Verify candidate A (res*a^b = X^Y) is true initially (res=1, a=X, b=Y so 1*X^Y = X^Y), then check both branches preserve it, then check it gives the correct answer when b=0.

steps.title: Check invariant holds initially (before first iteration)
steps.apply: Before the loop starts: res=1, a=X, b=Y. So res*a^b = 1 * X^Y = X^Y. The invariant holds initially.
steps.note: This is the base case of the invariant proof.

steps.title: Verify invariant is preserved in the even case
steps.apply: When b is even: a becomes a*a = a^2, b becomes b/2. New invariant value: res * (a^2)^(b/2) = res * a^(2 * b/2) = res * a^b = X^Y. Preserved.
steps.note: The key algebraic identity: (a^2)^(b/2) = a^b when b is even.

steps.title: Verify invariant is preserved in the odd case
steps.apply: When b is odd: res becomes res*a, b becomes b-1. New invariant value: (res*a) * a^(b-1) = res * a^1 * a^(b-1) = res * a^b = X^Y. Preserved.
steps.note: When b is odd, one factor of a is moved from a^b to res.

steps.title: Check correctness at loop exit
steps.apply: Loop exits when b=0. At this point res*a^0 = res*1 = res = X^Y by the invariant. So returning res gives X^Y. Correct.
steps.note: This confirms option A is the right loop invariant and the function is correct.

questions.formula_note: Binary exponentiation loop invariant: res * a^b = X^Y throughout the loop. Proof by showing it holds initially and is preserved by both the even and odd branches.

questions.to_find: The precondition on x, y, q, r that ensures the loop terminates with x == y*q + r

understand.plain: The C code fragment performs integer division of x by y by repeatedly subtracting y from r and incrementing q. We need to find what must be true BEFORE the loop runs to guarantee: (1) the loop terminates, and (2) at termination x == y*q + r. The loop invariant x = y*q + r must hold throughout — it holds initially if r=x and q=0, and y > 0 ensures termination.

keywords.explain: A condition that must be true before a code fragment runs to guarantee a desired postcondition after it finishes.
keywords.example: Precondition q==0 && r==x means: quotient starts at 0, remainder starts as x (the whole dividend).

keywords.explain: A loop terminates if the loop variant (some decreasing non-negative measure) is bounded below.
keywords.example: Here r decreases by y each iteration; if y > 0, r eventually falls below y and the loop stops.

keywords.explain: An unsigned integer can never be negative; if y==0, r-y could wrap around (undefined for unsigned), so y > 0 is essential.
keywords.example: If y=0, the loop condition r >= y is always true (r >= 0 always for unsigned), causing infinite loop.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='440' height='170'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='420' height='40' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='20' y='35' class='lbl ink'>Precondition: q==0  &&  r==x  &&  y>0</text><rect x='10' y='60' width='420' height='35' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='82' class='sub ink'>Invariant: x = y*q + r holds throughout (initially 1*0 + x = x ✓)</text><rect x='10' y='104' width='200' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='122' class='tag ink'>Each iteration:</text><text x='20' y='140' class='sub ink'>r := r - y  (r decreases)</text><text x='20' y='156' class='sub ink'>q := q + 1  (q increases by 1)</text><rect x='225' y='104' width='205' height='55' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='235' y='122' class='tag ink'>Terminates when r < y</text><text x='235' y='140' class='sub ink'>y > 0 ensures r eventually</text><text x='235' y='156' class='sub ink'>drops below y (no infinite loop)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Precondition q==0, r==x, y>0 initializes the loop invariant x = y*q + r (since y*0 + x = x). Each iteration preserves the invariant. y > 0 guarantees termination because r strictly decreases until r < y.

given.aim: Find the precondition that ensures the while loop terminates with x == y*q + r.

terms.meaning: The relationship between x, y, q, r that must hold throughout the loop
terms.example: x=17, y=5: initially q=0, r=17. After 1 iter: q=1, r=12. Check: 5*1+12=17 ✓
terms.connects: Must hold before loop (from precondition) and at termination (gives desired postcondition)

terms.meaning: Quotient starts at zero since no divisions have occurred yet
terms.example: q=0 before loop; x = y*0 + r = r, so we also need r = x
terms.connects: Together with r==x, ensures invariant holds at loop entry

terms.meaning: Remainder starts equal to the full dividend x
terms.example: r=x=17; as loop runs r shrinks and q grows maintaining 17 = 5*q + r
terms.connects: Establishes the invariant: x = y*0 + x = x. Correct.

terms.meaning: Divisor must be positive to guarantee termination
terms.example: If y=0, loop condition r>=0 is always true for unsigned int — infinite loop
terms.connects: Ensures r strictly decreases each iteration, so loop must eventually terminate

given.plan: For the postcondition x==y*q+r to hold at termination, the loop invariant x=y*q+r must be established before the loop. This requires q=0 and r=x. For termination with unsigned variables, we need y > 0 so r actually decreases. Option C gives all three conditions.

steps.title: Establish the required loop invariant
steps.apply: The desired postcondition is x == y*q + r. For this to hold at loop exit, x = y*q + r must be a loop invariant (true before, during, and after). To establish it before the loop, we need y*q + r = x. The simplest way: q=0 and r=x gives y*0 + x = x. Correct.
steps.note: If q is not 0 initially or r is not x, the invariant would not hold from the start.

steps.title: Check invariant is preserved by each loop iteration
steps.formula_raw: y*(q+1) + (r-y) = y*q + y + r - y = y*q + r = x
steps.apply: After r := r-y and q := q+1: new value of y*q+r = y*(q+1)+(r-y) = y*q+y+r-y = y*q+r = x. Invariant preserved.
steps.note: The invariant is an inductive property maintained by the loop body.

steps.title: Verify termination requires y > 0
steps.formula_raw: r decreases by y per iteration; terminates when r < y
steps.apply: Variables are unsigned int. If y=0, the condition r>=y becomes r>=0 which is always true for unsigned — infinite loop. If y>0, r decreases strictly each iteration. Since r is non-negative (unsigned), it must eventually fall below y and the loop exits.
steps.note: Option D (q==0 && r==x) omits y>0, so it does NOT guarantee termination.

steps.title: Evaluate all options
steps.apply: Option A: q==r && r==0 gives x = y*0+0 = 0, requires x=0. Too restrictive. Option B: x>0 && r==x && y>0 but q is unconstrained — if q!=0, invariant x=y*q+r = y*q+x != x unless q=0. Fails. Option C: q==0 && r==x && y>0 establishes invariant (y*0+x=x) and guarantees termination (y>0). Correct. Option D: q==0 && r==x but missing y>0; if y=0, no termination for unsigned.
steps.note: Option C is the minimal sufficient precondition.

questions.formula_note: Loop invariant method: to prove a loop computes x = y*q + r, establish the invariant before the loop (q=0, r=x), verify each iteration preserves it, and require y > 0 for termination with unsigned integer variables.
